pageHeaderText.pageHeaderText1: Choose the form of payment you want to receive on October 1, 2017. You can change your form of payment up to the last business day before your benefits begin. The forms of payment are based on other retirement choices you've made:

pageHeaderText2.point1: Your last day of employment will be September 18, 2017.
pageHeaderText2.point2: You'll begin receiving the payment you choose on October 1, 2017.
pageHeaderText2.point3: Chris M. Alvarez is the beneficiary chosen for this payment option.
pageHeaderText2.point4: No beneficiary is chosen for this plan.

pageHeaderText.noteLegalText: Note: The Premier Company reserves the right to correct any errors. If it's determined at any time that the information provided on this site conflicts with the benefit defined by the Premier Pension Plan, the Premier Pension Plan will prevail. Under the law, a plan must be operated in accordance with its terms.
pageHeaderText.contactlText: Review the details of all your payment options. If you have any questions, specialists are ready to help at 888-123-4567 (8 a.m. to 4 p.m. CT, Monday through Friday).
pageHeaderText.instructionText: You must choose 2 payment options for this benefit.
pageHeaderText.deferPanelText: You can skip (defer) making a pension choice and come back to make that choice later.
pageHeaderText.expandedDeferPanelText1: You may choose to defer payment of a pension benefit if:

expandedDeferPanelText2.point: You want to make your health care choices and prefer to begin pension benefits later.
You want to review your savings options and prefer to begin pension benefits later.
You (or your spouse, if applicable) have other sources of income and don't need your pension benefits now.
You don't want to pay taxes (if applicable) on your benefits until later.

pageHeaderText.benefitDeferModalHeader: Do you want to defer your payment?
pageHeaderText.benefitDeferModalText: If you want to begin to receive your pension benefit at a later date, choose Yes.

optionalFormDescriptionTable.relativeValueRollOverText:
Relative Value

While you must choose an option based on your personal situation, the relative value information shows how the value of each payment option compares to the values of the others. The relative value is an estimate that is determined by comparing the value of each payment option available to the value of the Single Life Annuity.

optionalFormDescriptionTable.optionalFormDescription: Age 62 Level Income and 100% Joint and Survivor Annuity
optionalFormDescriptionTable.panelHeaderText: A monthly payment to you until you die, then to a beneficiary, with an increased benefit until you reach (or would have reached) age 62, when your payment is reduced (because you can take or could have taken your Social Security benefit)

panelText.point: Receive retirement payments immediately if you retire early (between ages 55 and 62)
Similar amount of income before and after age 62
Increased benefit until you reach age 62 (and can take your Social Security benefit
Reduced benefit when you reach age 62 (because you can take your Social Security benefit)
If you die before age 62, beneficiary receives 100% of your payment, until you would have reached age 62 (when payment will be reduced, as it would have if you were still living)
If you die after age 62, 100% of what you would have received if you had chosen the regular 100% Joint and Survivor payment option will be paid to your beneficiary
If married, your spouse must consent

pageHeaderText.hypotheticalPaymentOptionText: Hypothetical payment options are available to you but you can't choose them now. We don't have all the information needed to calculate the actual amounts.
pageHeaderText.survivorBenefitText: To calculate hypothetical options that include a survivor benefit, we used an estimated beneficiary birth date of [mm-dd-yyyy]. To choose one of these options, you must name a beneficiary.
pageHeaderText.socialSecurityLevelText: To calculate hypothetical options that include a Social Security level income feature, we used an estimated Social Security benefit amount. To choose one of these options, contact the Premier Benefits Center at 1-800-XXX-XXXX. We'll tell you how to send us information so we can recalculate your payment options.

mainSectionContentList.content: [Premier Company] is legally required to provide you with relative value information for the payment options available to you. This information will help you compare the values of your payment options, relative to each other.

It's difficult to compare all your options because they may be paid over different periods of time. For example, some are paid monthly for only your lifetime and some are paid monthly for the lifetime of you and your spouse.

While you must choose an option based on your personal situation, the relative value information shows how the value of each payment option compares to the values of the others. The relative value information is only an estimate because the true relative value depends on how long you live.

planContentList.content: The payment option amounts for the [Premier Company Benefit election] are all about equal in relative value to the [Single Life Annuity]. This relative value comparison is made by comparing the value of each available payment option to the value of the [Single Life Annuity]. This comparison uses interest and average life expectancy assumptions.
An interest rate assumption of [5.00]% for the first 5 years, [10.00]% for years 5 through 19, and [15.00]% for years 20 and later was used for all optional forms of payment.
The actual relative value of payments made to you will depend on how long you live. If you want more information on the average life expectancies used in this calculation, Contact Us.

For the relative values comparison, we calculated your [50% Joint and Survivor Annuity] for the [Premier Company Benefit election] using your spouse as a beneficiary and using an estimated birth date of [February 12, 1950] for your spouse. You can provide the actual birth date for your spouse and receive an updated calculation. However, this request would require a recalculation of your payment options, which could change your benefit amount and may delay your payment.

pageHeaderText.expandSectionContentText1: You may be required to pay Social Security and Medicare taxes on the nonqualified pension payments that are shown on this site. These taxes, which are required by the Internal Revenue Service, are often called FICA (Federal Insurance Contributions Act) taxes. If FICA taxes apply to you, they'll be deducted from your active payroll, if applicable, or your payment. The timing of the withholding may vary.
pageHeaderText.contentText: In addition to FICA taxes, federal and state tax withholding may also apply to your nonqualified [pension] payment.
pageHeaderText.expandSectionContentText2: Your cash balance benefit has been calculated using current interest rates. Your actual cash balance benefit will be converted to the annuity options based on the interest rate in effect on your payment start date. If the interest rate at the time your payment begins is different from the current rate, your annuity options may be higher or lower than the amounts shown on this site.
pageHeaderText.contentText1: Your cash balance benefit has been calculated based on:

expandSectionContentConditionText2.expandSectionPoint: The value of your account from this plan
Any benefit you accrued from a previous plan

pageHeaderText.expandSectionContentText3: According to our records, a portion of your benefit may be assigned to an alternate payee due to a Qualified Domestic Relations Order (QDRO). The benefit shown hasn't been adjusted to reflect this QDRO.
pageHeaderText.footnoteText2: 2 This is a hypothetical estimate. Refer to the Hypothetical Payment Options section for a further explanation.
pageHeaderText.quitButtonModalText:
What do you want to do?

Save
Keep the choices you've made, so far. Finish making pension choices later.

Quit
Cancel your pension request and lose all the pension choices you've made, so far.

Return
Go back to the current page without quitting.
pageHeaderText.peopleLikeMeText: Find out what choices others are making.
pageHeaderText.questionsBoxText:
Questions?
If or when you have questions, specialists are ready to help at 888-123-4567 (8 a.m. to 4:30 p.m. CT, Monday through Friday).
pageHeaderText.resourceLinkText2: Information Used to Calculate Your Pension Benefit
